title: Hedge
author: Jane Delury
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Hedge" book by Jane Delury?
Options:
A. This novel was part romance, part mystery thriller that had me totally engrossed up until the very last page. It was a heartwarming, exhilarating, and riveting read! Jean Kwok, author of Searching for Sylvie Lee A poignant examination of how fragile family bonds can be and the inherent struggle between being a mom and pursuing one's passions. I was completely transfixed and could not put it down. Deeply absorbing and tremendously gratifying. Fiona Davis, author of The Spectacular In the magnificent Hedge, Delury thoughtfully explores the intricacies of married life, raising kids, and creative work. Maud is an everyday woman who we can relate to and who is strong-willed, and she comes to a turning point. An unforgettable character in a profoundly affecting and honest story.
B. This book was part love story and part thriller that kept me totally immersed until the final page. It was a feel-good, exciting, and captivating read! Jean Kwok, author of Searching for Sylvie Lee A wonderful examination of how fragile family relationships can be and the natural tension between motherhood and personal desires. I was completely transfixed and could not put it down. Deeply engrossing and extremely satisfying. Fiona Davis, author of The Spectacular In the brilliant Hedge, Delury delves into the complexities of matrimony, raising children, and the creative process. Maud is an ordinary woman who is easy to relate to and strong-willed, and she comes to a crossroads. An unforgettable protagonist in a profoundly moving and authentic tale.
C. Part love story, part thriller, this novel kept me entranced until the very last page. A warm, glorious, and exciting read! Jean Kwok, author of Searching for Sylvie Lee A beautiful exploration of the fragility of family life and the inherent tension between motherhood and desire. I simply could not put it down. Transfixing and utterly rewarding. Fiona Davis, author of The Spectacular The magnificent Delury explores the complexities of marriage, parenthood, and art in Hedge. Maud is an everywoman, relatable and strong, who  finds herself at a crossroads.  An unforgettable character in a story of arresting beauty and truth.
D. This captivating novel was part romance and part mystery that gripped me to the very end. It was a heartwarming, thrilling, and engaging read! Jean Kwok, writer of Searching for Sylvie Lee An exquisite look at the delicate nature of family life and the inherent struggle between being a mother and following one's passions. I was completely hooked and could not put it down. Utterly absorbing and extremely rewarding. Fiona Davis, author of The Spectacular In the magnificent Hedge, Delury examines the intricacies of marriage, parenting, and creativity. Maud is an everyday woman who is relatable and strong, and finds herself at a turning point. An unforgettable character in a profoundly beautiful and honest story.
